headline: Agentic systems anchored the month as production pipelines turned AI agents from chat assistants into code-shipping infrastructure.
body: Agentic systems and AI agents led the rotation, with PostHog detailing a pipeline that converts product signals like errors and session events into pull requests using background agents, embedding LLM-generated queries for semantic clustering and folding test-time compute into one-shot calls [3]. Persistent context tooling advanced in parallel: PAI builds a Life OS on Claude Code with persistent memory, custom skills, and structured workflows to hold context across sessions [0], while Claude Design extends the same stack into automated design generation [2]. On the model side, Gemma 4's 31B entrant placed fourth on the LM Arena open leaderboard while running on a single GPU and on-device, underscoring token efficiency in smaller foundation models [1]. A distributed-training cluster of new entrants broke out, including DeepSpeed Ulysses, Untied Ulysses, CPU offloading, and auto-scaling, alongside a separate cautionary case as lawyers were sanctioned for AI-generated citations to nonexistent cases [4].

cited_items.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
How to Use Claude Design – Full AI Design Workflow 2026
Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
